sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 20:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 20:1
items.html: The Sefirot incorporate with one another “in passing” (see answer 15). Since the ten Sefirot of direct light are extended from Keter to Malchut, it is impossible for them to appear in the degree, except by clothing in the ten Sefirot of reflected light that are extended and rise from Malchut to Keter. Thus, there is not a single Sefira among them that does not have two Sefirot from its own phase, one of direct light and one of reflected light, as well as eight Sefirot that were incorporated in it in passing. Some of them passed through it in passing from above downward, and some of them passed through it in passing from below upward.
